specification.topic-statement: The OpenAI Chat Completions streaming system processes server-sent events from the Chat Completions API and emits normalized stream events in real time.

scope.in-scope: SSE line parsing, JSON event dispatch, lazy START emission, text delta forwarding, finish reason accumulation, usage extraction, DONE marker handling, in-stream error detection, and final response assembly.
scope.boundary: Raw bytes arrive from the HTTP layer via a write callback. The callback receives a chunk of bytes and a length. This topic processes those bytes. The completion callback is invoked by the HTTP layer after the transfer ends.
This topic performs its own manual SSE line extraction rather than delegating to the shared SSE parser. The shared SSE parser is used by other providers.
When the model is routed to the Responses API, the write callback delegates all processing to the Responses streaming system. This specification covers only the Chat Completions path.
Parsed events are delivered to an external callback. This topic does not define what the consumer does with the events.

field.name: model
field.note: null until extracted from first event

field.name: finish reason
field.note: default UNKNOWN; updated by each delta

field.name: started
field.note: false until first text delta triggers START

field.name: content blocks
field.note: null or single tool call block

field.name: arguments
field.note: defaults to "{}" if accumulated args were null

behavior.sse-line-parsing:
When bytes arrive from the HTTP layer, they are appended to an internal line buffer. The buffer is scanned for newline characters. Each complete line (terminated by a newline) is examined. Lines beginning with the prefix "data: " have the prefix stripped and the remainder is treated as an SSE event payload. Lines without this prefix are ignored.
After extracting all complete lines, any remaining partial line (bytes after the last newline) is preserved in the buffer for the next chunk. When no partial line remains, the buffer is released entirely.
Only newline ('\n') is used as the line terminator. Carriage returns are not stripped. If server sends '\r\n' line endings, the '\r' remains in the extracted payload.
behavior.event-dispatch:
Each extracted SSE payload is dispatched to the event processor. The processor first checks whether the payload is the literal string "[DONE]". If so, the done-marker path executes. Otherwise, the payload is parsed as JSON. If JSON parsing fails, the event is silently discarded and processing continues with the next event.
For successfully parsed JSON, the processor checks in order: 1. Whether the root object contains an "error" field — if so, the error path executes. 2. Whether the model has been extracted yet — if not, it is extracted now. 3. Whether there is a choices array with at least one choice containing a delta. 4. Whether there is a usage object.
If the SSE payload is not valid JSON, the event is silently dropped. No error is emitted. Streaming continues with subsequent events.
When the root JSON object contains an "error" field whose value is an object, the error object is processed immediately. The message field and type field are extracted from it. The type string is mapped to an error category: - "authentication" or "permission" → AUTH - "rate_limit" → RATE_LIMIT - "invalid_request" → INVALID_ARG - "server" or "service" → SERVER - All other values → UNKNOWN
An ERROR stream event is emitted with the category and message. After the error event, processing of the current JSON document stops. No further events from this document are emitted.
When the stream delivers an error inside a JSON event, a DONE event is never emitted for that stream. The HTTP transfer still completes normally, and the completion callback is still invoked.
behavior.model-extraction:
The model identifier is extracted from the first event that contains a non-null "model" field. Once set, the model is not updated by subsequent events. The model is stored and later included in the START event and the built response.
If the first event lacks a model field, the model remains null until a later event provides it. If no event provides it, the model is null in the final response.
behavior.lazy-start-emission:
The START stream event is not emitted at the beginning of the HTTP transfer. Instead, it is deferred until the first text content delta arrives. This is tracked by a boolean flag initialized to false at context creation.
When a text delta is about to be emitted and the flag is false, the START event is emitted first with the model identifier (which may be null if not yet extracted). The flag is then set to true. All subsequent text deltas emit only TEXT_DELTA events.
If the stream produces no text content deltas — for example, if the stream consists only of an error event, or if the stream ends before any content arrives — the START event is never emitted. Any consumer that requires a START event before processing deltas must handle the absence of START.
The Chat Completions API code paths for tool calls do not call the lazy-start check. A stream that produces only tool call deltas and no text content would never emit a START event. However, tool call paths in Chat Completions streaming are excluded from active use, so this path is not currently reachable.
behavior.text-delta-processing:
When a delta object in the choices array contains a "content" field with a string value, the lazy-start check fires (emitting START if not yet emitted), and then a TEXT_DELTA event is emitted with that string value.
If the content field is absent, null, or not a string, no TEXT_DELTA is emitted for that delta. Processing continues normally.
The text pointer delivered in the TEXT_DELTA event points directly into the parsed JSON document, which is valid only for the duration of the callback.
The text value is not copied before emission. If the consumer retains the pointer after the callback returns, the behavior is undefined.
behavior.finish-reason-accumulation:
Each delta object may contain a "finish_reason" field. If present and non-null, its string value is mapped to an internal finish reason and stored in the context, replacing any previously stored value. The mapping is: - "stop" → STOP - "length" → LENGTH - "tool_calls" → TOOL_USE - "content_filter" → CONTENT_FILTER - "error" → ERROR - null or any other value → UNKNOWN
The stored finish reason is used when the DONE event is emitted.
behavior.done-marker-processing:
When the SSE payload is the literal string "[DONE]", the done path executes. Any in-progress tool call state is finalized. A DONE stream event is emitted with the accumulated finish reason and usage counters. No further stream events are emitted after the DONE event.
The [DONE] marker and the usage-carrying event typically arrive together in the same HTTP chunk, but usage is extracted from the JSON chunk that precedes [DONE]. If no usage event was delivered before [DONE], all token counts are zero in the DONE event.
behavior.usage-extraction:
When a JSON event contains a "usage" field that is an object, the following fields are extracted and stored in the context, overwriting any previously stored values: - "prompt_tokens" → input token count - "completion_tokens" → output token count - "completion_tokens_details.reasoning_tokens" → thinking token count - "total_tokens" → total token count
If any field is absent or is not a number, the corresponding counter retains its previous value (zero by default).
If "completion_tokens_details" is absent, the thinking token count remains zero.
behavior.completion-callback:
After the HTTP transfer completes, the completion callback is invoked. The callback assembles the final response from the accumulated streaming context and delivers it to the caller.
The final response contains: - The model identifier (may be null) - The final accumulated finish reason - The final accumulated usage counters - A content blocks array: if a tool call was accumulated (both tool ID and tool name are non-null), a single TOOL_CALL block is created with the accumulated ID, name, arguments, and a null thought_signature. If no tool call was accumulated, the content blocks array is null with count zero.
If the accumulated arguments for a tool call are null, the arguments field in the content block defaults to "{}".
The Chat Completions streaming path does not emit TOOL_CALL_START or TOOL_CALL_DELTA events and does not accumulate tool call state. The thought_signature in the response is always null. The content blocks array in the response is always null with count zero for Chat API responses in the current implementation.
The Chat Completions streaming path does not emit THINKING_DELTA events. Thinking content is not present in Chat Completions responses. The thinking token count in usage is sourced from completion_tokens_details.reasoning_tokens if present.
